title: Markets stabilise amid US-Iran peace talks
section_path: Markets stabilise amid US-Iran peace talks
section_path_text: Markets stabilise amid US-Iran peace talks
summary:
Main
Energy markets were relatively subdued as they contemplate the impact of US-Iran peace talks. Improved risk appetite pushed industrial metals higher. Precious metals fell on concerns that rising inflation will keep rates high.

children.title: Main
children.summary: Energy markets were relatively subdued as they contemplate the impact of US-Iran peace talks. Improved risk appetite pushed industrial metals higher.

children.title: Market Commentary
children.summary: Crude oil prices fluctuated on the prospect of fresh peace talks between the US and Iran. The two parties are reported to be considering extending their ceasefire by another two weeks to allow for more time to negotiate a peace agreement, according to a Bloomberg report.

children.title: Chart of the Day
children.summary: The US has started releasing oil from its strategic petroleum reserve as promised. As part of the International Energy Agency's plan to help soften the blow of the hit to Persian Gulf oil supplies, the US agreed to release 172mbbls.
